costs: Exam redesign and psychometric validation costs
Administrative burden of SES-proxy verification and algorithm changes
content.equity_impact: Direct and immediate, but partial: targets the entry gate specifically while leaving intact the upstream primary-school choice segregation and downstream track-based inequality; expected to reduce but not eliminate the 43.6%-vs-9.2% type composition gaps
expected_benefits: Reduced weight of purchased coaching relative to underlying ability in determining admission (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05)
Potential narrowing of the stark parental-education composition gap documented since 2000 (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04)
Preserves existing legal structure, avoiding constitutional confrontation over free school choice (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03, F-live-legal_and_governance-02)
content.goal: Reduce family-capital-driven sorting at the point of gimnázium entry without altering the 6/8-year track structure itself

implementation_steps.action: Commission redesign of maths/Hungarian exam items toward reasoning over coachable pattern-recognition, pilot with psychometric coachability testing
Issue regulation adding SES-based proxy weighting (e.g., school-level disadvantage index bonus) into the national ranking algorithm
Mandate that every primary school actively register and prepare eligible disadvantaged students for the exam, tracked via participation audits
Publish annual audit of admitted-cohort SES composition vs. baseline to monitor effect

mechanisms: National uniform exam design changes to reduce coaching-return (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02, F-live-hungarian_education_system-05)
SES-aware quota or bonus-point proxies layered onto the existing centralized deferred-acceptance algorithm (F-live-legal_and_governance-03)
Capping interview weight already regulated at 25% and further limiting subjective/coachable components (F-live-legal_and_governance-03)
Address disadvantaged-school non-participation in the entry competition by mandating outreach/registration support (F-live-equity_and_social_mobility-03, F-live-education_finance-02)
risks: Elite families adapt coaching strategies to new exam format, eroding gains over time
Gimnázium directors, an organized interest group with direct political access, may resist changes perceived as diluting selectivity (F-live-political_feasibility-07)
SES proxies could be legally contested as discriminatory absent explicit legislative basis (F-live-legal_and_governance-02)
Does not address segregation originating in primary-school choice, a documented independent mechanism (F-live-portuguese_reform-06, F-live-polish_reform-07)
content.title: Coaching-resistant, SES-weighted admissions reform within the existing track structure
